seoTitle: Craigie vs Cramphorne — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Craigie and Cramphorne. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Craigie and Cramphorne.
heading: Craigie vs Cramphorne — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Craigie, Western Australia and Cramphorne, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Cramphorne has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Craigie (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Craigie or Cramphorne safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Craigie has a flood risk score of 56/100, while Cramphorne scores 46/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Craigie scores 50/100 for bushfire risk. Cramphorne scores 18/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Craigie or Cramphorne?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Cramphorne has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.5% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
